description: Synthetic raw findings fixture for consolidator benchmark. Simulates output from 6 validators (1 Enum + 1 Adv per lens × 3 lenses) on pipeline-arch-multi-iter.md. Designed to test consolidator deduplication, confidence tagging, and hallucination filtering.

structural_enum.description: Direct contradiction: Overview states 'All pipeline stages run in parallel' while Stage 2 states 'Stage 2 must complete before Stage 3 begins'.
structural_enum.evidence: Overview: 'All pipeline stages run in parallel to maximize throughput.' Stage 2: 'Stage 2 must complete before Stage 3 begins.'

structural_enum.description: Dangling reference: 'Security considerations are documented in Section 6.' No Section 6 exists in this document.
structural_enum.evidence: 'Security considerations are documented in Section 6.' Document ends at Conclusions with no Section 6 present.

structural_enum.description: Authentication type unspecified: 'Authentication is used to verify each request' with no mechanism named.
structural_enum.evidence: 'Authentication is used to verify each request before processing begins.' No type (API key, OAuth, mTLS) specified.

structural_adv.description: The document contradicts itself on execution topology — parallel claim in Overview vs sequential dependency in Stage 2.
structural_adv.evidence: 'All pipeline stages run in parallel' (Overview) contradicts 'Stage 2 must complete before Stage 3 begins' (Stage 2).

structural_adv.description: Reference to non-existent Section 6 for security documentation.
structural_adv.evidence: 'Security considerations are documented in Section 6.' No such section exists.

structural_adv.description: Conclusions reference 'adding a real-time streaming workflow alongside the existing batch pipeline' as a future enhancement, but the current document describes the system as already handling both batch and streaming — this reads as a contradiction in scope.
structural_adv.evidence: 'Future enhancements include adding a real-time streaming workflow alongside the existing batch pipeline.' Stage 2 already discusses both batch and streaming processing modes.

structural_adv.description: The document references SLA targets in Stage 3 that are contradicted by the Performance Characteristics section.
structural_adv.evidence: Stage 3 mentions SLA targets — no such text exists in Stage 3.

accuracy_enum.description: Batch/streaming performance claim is factually inverted. Streaming is more efficient for small files; batch incurs setup overhead that yields no benefit at small sizes.
accuracy_enum.evidence: 'Benchmarks show that batch processing is 3× faster than streaming for files under 10MB.' Standard data engineering: streaming outperforms batch at small sizes.

accuracy_enum.description: 99.9% latency reduction claim is implausible without supporting data.
accuracy_enum.evidence: 'The in-memory delivery cache reduces end-to-end latency by 99.9% compared to unbuffered writes.' No baseline, conditions, or methodology.

accuracy_enum.description: Benchmark claim unattributed — 'Benchmarks show' with no source, methodology, or conditions.
accuracy_enum.evidence: 'Benchmarks show that batch processing is 3× faster than streaming for files under 10MB.'

accuracy_enum.description: The 5-minute polling interval in Stage 4 contradicts the sub-second latency claim in Stage 3 — you cannot achieve sub-second end-to-end latency while polling every 5 minutes.
accuracy_enum.evidence: Stage 4: 'The monitoring system polls health endpoints every 5 minutes.' Stage 3: 'reduces end-to-end latency by 99.9%' (implying sub-second delivery).

accuracy_adv.description: The batch/streaming performance claim reads backwards — batch is not faster than streaming for small files in standard architectures.
accuracy_adv.evidence: 'batch processing is 3× faster than streaming for files under 10MB' — contradicts standard knowledge: streaming wins at small sizes due to batch setup overhead.

accuracy_adv.description: 99.9% latency reduction is an extraordinary claim with no backing evidence.
accuracy_adv.evidence: 'reduces end-to-end latency by 99.9%' with no methodology, baseline, or citation.
'Benchmarks show that batch processing is 3× faster than streaming for files under 10MB.' No source named.

coherence_enum.description: 'Pipeline' and 'workflow' used interchangeably and inconsistently — same sentence treats them as distinct, Conclusions treats them as distinct entities.
coherence_enum.evidence: Operational Notes: 'The pipeline and workflow are monitored.' Conclusions: 'a real-time streaming workflow alongside the existing batch pipeline.' No definitions provided.

coherence_enum.description: 'Standard retry policy' referenced but never defined.
coherence_enum.evidence: 'Transformation applies the standard retry policy on transient failures.' No definition of retry count, backoff, or timeout.

coherence_enum.description: 'Large files' handled differently but threshold undefined.
coherence_enum.evidence: 'Large files are handled separately from the primary transformation path via an overflow queue.' No size threshold specified.

coherence_enum.description: ETL acronym not expanded on first use.
coherence_enum.evidence: 'an internal ETL system' — ETL not expanded.

coherence_adv.description: Pipeline/workflow terminology drift — document never settles on which term means what.
coherence_adv.evidence: Operational Notes: 'pipeline and workflow' as if distinct. Conclusions: 'streaming workflow...batch pipeline' as if distinct in a different way.

coherence_adv.description: 'Standard retry policy' is a named thing that goes undefined.
coherence_adv.evidence: 'applies the standard retry policy' — no definition anywhere in document.

coherence_adv.description: Operational Notes and Stage 4 Monitoring section repeat substantially the same information about the Prometheus dashboard.
coherence_adv.evidence: Stage 4: 'The monitoring system uses a Prometheus dashboard.' Operational Notes bullet 1: 'Monitored via the Prometheus dashboard.' Near-duplicate.

notes.hallucination: SA-HAL-001 — references SLA text that does not exist in Stage 3. Consolidator should discard this after investigating.
